title: galarhoeus pekinensis subsp. fauriei
intro: Galarhoeus pekinensis subsp. fauriei, also known as Korean milkwort or Cheonggye stream plant, is a type of plant native to East Asia and is a subspecies of Galarhoeus pekinensis. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Korea and China, for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as having potential anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Galarhoeus pekinensis subsp. fauriei is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in combination with other herbs in traditional Korean and Chinese medicine.
